title: Lumnitzera lutea
taxonomy: Lumnitzera lutea is a species of flowering plant in the family Combretaceae. It is commonly known as the yellow mangrove or red mangrove. The plant is native to the tropical regions of Asia and Australia. Its classification includes being a part of the order Myrtales, a large and diverse order of plants. Lumnitzera lutea is also known as a mangrove species, which is a group of trees and shrubs that thrive in coastal ecosystems.
uses: Lumnitzera lutea has been found to possess various medicinal properties, with traditional uses including treating fever, cough, and respiratory issues. The plant's extracts have been reported to exhibit antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory activities, which may contribute to its medicinal effects. Additionally, Lumnitzera lutea has been used in traditional medicine to treat digestive problems and skin conditions.
